code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test23-MAY-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, indicating possible renal impairment. Urinalysis reveals significant proteinuria, bilirubinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low specific gravity and abnormal urine color/appearance, suggesting impaired renal function and possible tubular damage. HbA1c is low, which may reflect recent hypoglycemia or analytical interference. Further nephrological evaluation and clinical correlation are recommended.
